classification_basis.canonical_cluster_rediscovery: The line identifies an MCP, managed/scheduled agent, hosted skill or agent upload, external-tool handoff, or typed orchestration boundary already represented by MERGE-004. Declaration and code capability are not treated as evidence of deployed traffic, retention, training, or cross-customer reuse.
classification_basis.false_positive: The regex matched ordinary legal notification language, a heading, a local case or workflow handoff, or another use of 'handoff' that does not itself identify a remote or hosted source-to-sink boundary.
classification_basis.counterevidence: The line narrows or disables the apparent boundary, including explicit local-only operation, no proactive notification, unavailable-connector fallback, human-operated external tooling, or removal of plugin-command handoffs from a deliverable.
classification_basis.genuinely_new_material_pattern_or_source: Reserved for a materially different evidence class or source-to-sink mechanism not already represented by the six round-1 canonical clusters. No assigned record met this standard.

canonical_cluster.title: Remote connector and hosted-agent trust boundaries

runtime_boundary: No runtime execution, tenant configuration, credentials, traffic, retention, training, telemetry, or cross-customer reuse is inferred from these static matches.

saturation_recommendation.recommendation: GEN-003 and GEN-004 add no new material pattern class or source-to-sink candidate beyond MERGE-004. Treat this targeted lane as saturated, subject to independent aggregate reconciliation with the other round-2 lanes.
saturation_recommendation.scope_limit: This recommendation covers only the 336 GEN-003/GEN-004 records at the pinned revision; it does not close runtime, contract, telemetry, or incomplete history proof gaps.
